definitions: To discard or get rid of something unwanted.
To expel someone from a place or group.
To produce or emit something, often quickly or in large quantities.
To suggest an idea or plan for consideration.
To dislocate a body part, especially one's back.
examples: Please throw out the old newspapers.
The bouncer had to throw out the disruptive customer.
The volcano began to throw out ash and smoke.
She decided to throw out a new proposal during the meeting.
He threw out his back lifting that heavy box.
